differences.title: 1. RENDERING STRATEGY
differences.leftDesc: Server-side rendering and Incremental Static Regeneration are core.
differences.rightDesc: Generates static HTML by default, can hydrate selectively.

differences.title: 2. JavaScript LOAD
differences.leftDesc: Runs JavaScript on the client by default for interactivity.
differences.rightDesc: Ships zero JavaScript to the client unless explicitly hydrated.

differences.title: 3. DATA FETCHING
differences.leftDesc: Flexible data fetching on server or client sides.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily fetches data on the server during build time.

differences.title: 4. OPTIMIZATION
differences.leftDesc: Built-in components for image and script optimization.

differences.title: 5. FRAMEWORK TYPE
differences.leftDesc: Leverages React ecosystem for dynamic features.
differences.rightDesc: Framework-agnostic, focuses on content delivery performance.

differences.title: 6. USE CASE
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for highly interactive web applications.
differences.rightDesc: Best for blogs, marketing sites, and documentation.

differences.title: 7. INTEGRATION
differences.leftDesc: Deep integration with React components and patterns.
differences.rightDesc: Supports multiple UI frameworks or plain HTML/JS.
